lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Zanik, the council is still very concerned about the city's safety. If you hear of any more H.A.M. activity on the surface, you must let me know.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm sure [player name] will help keep us safe from the H.A.M. people!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: And another thing. If you must visit the surface, the council asks that you be careful not to offend the surface-dwellers' customs.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Of course, I'll be careful.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Remember in particular to scrupulously observe the Lumbridge-Dorgeshuun treaty...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Alright!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh, hello, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Zanik! What have you been up to?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: How are your studies going, Oldak?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Oh! Zanik! It's fascinating. These 'runes' store a type of energy I've never encountered before.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Yes. Each type of rune has its power tuned to a different effect, but they are all essentially the same energy.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: What I want to know is where this energy came from. The stone that these runes were cut from must be infused with an enormous amount of raw magical energy, but where did that energy come from?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Maybe someday we'll find out! Oh, hello, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Zanik! What have you been up to?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Tell me again about the surface. You said the cavern ceiling was how high?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No, there was no cavern ceiling. Just a great expanse of blue sky!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: You couldn't see how high the ceiling was because the sky was in the way. I see.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No, you don't understand. There was NO ceiling. The surface is right on top of the world! There's no more rock above it!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: I'm still having trouble getting my head around this 'surface' idea.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Perhaps I should take you to see it?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no. I think I'd rather stay here and write about it.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Alright. Hello, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Zanik! What have you been up to?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hello, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Zanik! What have you been up to?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: A giant frog found its way into the mines, but I helped the guards to drive it off!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It got away in the end. But it was the biggest I'd ever seen!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I've been thinking about my destiny...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What was it Juna said to me? 'Your destiny is to lead all goblins into a new age.'

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: 'All goblins.' Not just the Dorgeshuun, all goblins...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: And what does it even mean, my destiny? Is it something I'm certain to do, or just something I ought to do? How can I know?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I talked to Juna about it, but she wouldn't tell me anything. She just said, 'You will know, when the time is right.'

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I've been helping Oldak with his experiments!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I took his magic ball all over the caves and even to Lumbridge to test what happened when I broke it, and I always ended up back here.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You might find one of those balls useful if you wanted to visit me! Perhaps you should talk to Oldak.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I went on another visit to Lumbridge! I still can't convince other Dorgeshuun to go to the surface with me, though.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You've still got that ancient mace...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It must be my imagination, but I can almost feel the power coming off it, like it's calling out to me.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You should keep it safe, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I thought you had the ancient goblin mace, [player name]. But I found it in the caves.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: When I picked it up I felt strange... it was as if someone was watching to me, urging me to do something, but I don't know what!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I gave it to Tegdak. If you want to have it again you should talk to him.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I visited the goblin village again. I wanted to learn more about their legend of the 'Chosen Commander'.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But I didn't find out any more. They couldn't decide whether I really was the Chosen Commander or not, and they just got into a big argument about it.
